verses.text: The king gives Esther Haman's house, and Mordecai, now revealed as her kin, receives the royal ring once worn by Haman. The despised Jew is exalted in the traitor's place. God lifts the faithful into the very honour their enemies forfeit.

The king gives Esther Haman's house, and Mordecai, now revealed as her kin, receives the royal ring once worn by Haman. The despised Jew is exalted in the traitor's place. God lifts the faithful into the very honour their enemies forfeit.

Esther falls weeping at the king's feet, pleading to undo Haman's murderous letters: 'how can I endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people?' Her own safety is not enough. Love for her people will not rest while they remain in danger.

Esther falls weeping at the king's feet, pleading to undo Haman's murderous letters: 'how can I endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people?' Her own safety is not enough. Love for her people will not rest while they remain in danger.

Esther falls weeping at the king's feet, pleading to undo Haman's murderous letters: 'how can I endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people?' Her own safety is not enough. Love for her people will not rest while they remain in danger.

Esther falls weeping at the king's feet, pleading to undo Haman's murderous letters: 'how can I endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people?' Her own safety is not enough. Love for her people will not rest while they remain in danger.

The king bids Mordecai and Esther write a new decree in his name, sealed with the ring, for what is so sealed none can reverse. A way is opened where law seemed to bar it. God can bring deliverance even within the bounds of an unchangeable command.

The king bids Mordecai and Esther write a new decree in his name, sealed with the ring, for what is so sealed none can reverse. A way is opened where law seemed to bar it. God can bring deliverance even within the bounds of an unchangeable command.

The scribes write to every province in its own language, and swift royal posts carry the decree throughout the empire. The good news is sped to all. When God turns the tide, He sees that word of rescue reaches the farthest of His people.

The scribes write to every province in its own language, and swift royal posts carry the decree throughout the empire. The good news is sped to all. When God turns the tide, He sees that word of rescue reaches the farthest of His people.

The decree grants the Jews the right to gather and defend their lives against any who would assault them on the appointed day. The threatened are armed to stand. God provides not only deliverance but the means to meet the danger with courage.

The decree grants the Jews the right to gather and defend their lives against any who would assault them on the appointed day. The threatened are armed to stand. God provides not only deliverance but the means to meet the danger with courage.

The decree is published everywhere, and the couriers are hastened on the king's business. No time is lost in spreading hope. The errands of mercy deserve the same urgency the world gives to its own affairs.

The decree is published everywhere, and the couriers are hastened on the king's business. No time is lost in spreading hope. The errands of mercy deserve the same urgency the world gives to its own affairs.

Mordecai goes out in royal blue and white, the city shouts for joy, and the Jews have light and gladness, while many peoples join them in fear. Mourning is turned to feasting. When God exalts His servant, the sorrow of His people becomes singing.
